challengePrompts: Rohan experiences sudden dizziness upon standing, and a look in the mirror shows his lips and fingernail beds look unusually pale.
Rohan feels constantly exhausted after minor activities, and a laboratory report confirms his circulating hemoglobin levels are lower than normal.
Rohan complains of persistent cold hands and feet, which his doctor explains is due to a drop in his body's total red cell count.
Rohan feels lightheaded while climbing stairs, and his laboratory chart shows a severe drop in his overall hematocrit percentage.
Rohan's blood test reveals microcytic and hypochromic red cells that physically lack the iron required to bind and carry enough oxygen.
Rohan frequently stumbles over objects in a dimly lit room, though his vision is absolutely flawless during the bright daylight hours.
Rohan complains that his eyes feel dry, gritty, and irritated, showing signs of severe drying on the cornea and conjunctiva.
Rohan struggles to find his seat when entering a dark movie theater, completely failing to adapt to the darkness.
Rohan complains of severe dryness under his eyelids, making it impossible to produce normal tears to lubricate the outer lens.
Rohan's teacher notices that his photoreceptive rod cells fail to function, preventing him from seeing clearly once the sun goes down.
Rohan complains of deep, aching skeletal pain in his legs, and his knees physically knock together when walking due to a soft matrix.
Rohan's legs show a visible outward bowing curvature, making it highly uncomfortable to bear weight while standing or running.
Rohan's pediatric doctor notices soft areas along his skeletal growth plates and a severe delay in tooth eruption.
Rohan experiences frequent wrist fractures from simple playground slips, signaling a major loss in skeletal mineral density.
Rohan has an incredibly slow recovery from a hairline fracture and complains of deep soreness within his skeletal framework.
Rohan's chest feels tight and he gasps for air because a loss of alveolar surface area slows down his internal gas exchange rate.
Rohan experiences shallow, rapid breathing and a dry bark-like cough centered entirely within his tight bronchial airways.
Rohan catches respiratory infections frequently, leaving his upper and lower respiratory tracts inflamed with a distinct wheezing sound.
Rohan cannot force enough air out to blow out a candle because his respiratory passages have lost their elastic recoil.
Rohan struggles to take deep breaths, complaining that a drop in the rate of carbon dioxide clearance makes him feel suffocated.
Rohan experiences a burning sensation in his upper abdomen shortly after eating, caused by excess hydrochloric acid irritating his tissue.
Rohan complains of painful acid reflux and a sour taste in his throat when his lower esophageal sphincter fails to keep gastric juice down.
Rohan avoids eating because it triggers sharp, burning cramps in the upper middle area where gastric ulcers are forming.
Rohan complains of an intense churning feeling in his upper belly, indicating a major failure in his pepsin-dependent chemical digestion.
Rohan feels a heavy rush of stomach acid rising directly into his throat whenever he lies down horizontally after lunch.
Rohan finds it extremely difficult to concentrate on a single task and forgets spoken instructions given mere minutes ago by his teacher.
Rohan complains of persistent brain fog, sluggish synaptic transmission speed, and an absolute inability to memorize simple formulas.
Rohan zones out during lectures and struggles with the executive function needed to organize his daily school schedule.
Rohan experiences slow motor responses and coordination errors when the master organ of the central nervous system faces mental exhaustion.
Rohan's mother notices he is highly irritable, loses cognitive focus instantly, and has poor short-term recall of yesterday's events.
Rohan has red, itchy patches on his elbows and cheeks that flare up immediately when his outer epidermal lipid barrier dries out.
Rohan's hands develop painful cracks and rough, scaling surfaces on his body's outermost cutaneous layer.
Rohan develops a raised, peeling, photosensitive rash on his arms after spending a hot afternoon outdoors without melanin protection.
Rohan complains that his outer epithelial seal is flaking off, leaving his body highly sensitive and vulnerable to bacterial entry.
Rohan's lips are chapped and bleeding, and dry, scaly rashes have spread across the entire external surface area of his arms.
Rohan's chest pounds rapidly and irregularly because a sudden drop in blood potassium has disrupted his cardiac contraction rhythm.
Rohan's central muscular pump experiences severe strain and ventricular hypertrophy from constantly pushing against high arterial blood pressure.
Rohan's specialized pacemaking node fails to maintain a steady, rhythmic pulse, causing a fluttering sensation behind his breastbone.
Rohan feels his pulse racing at an exhausting rate even when sitting quietly at his desk, accompanied by heavy palpitations.
Rohan's doctor detects a murmur and notes that narrowing coronary vessels are reducing the oxygen supply to his thick myocardial walls.
Rohan is eating a calorie-rich diet but continues to lose weight because the microscopic absorbing villi in his gut are severely blunted.
Rohan passes loose, greasy stools frequently because his damaged mucosal lining completely fails to absorb amino acids and fatty acids.
Rohan's nails show white spots because his brush-border enzymes are impaired, preventing the active transport of digested nutrients.
Rohan experiences loud rumbling sounds and chronic watery stools strictly caused by a failure to absorb micronutrients in his upper gut.
Rohan's growth curve has flattened due to an inability to absorb fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K) across his digestive lining walls.
Rohan has not passed stool for four days because his lower digestive tract is extracting too much water from his indigestible waste.
Rohan passes small, dry, hard fecal pellets with severe straining because a lack of dietary fiber has slowed his colonic transit time.
Rohan's lower belly feels hard, and a massive disruption in his colonic gut microbiome has led to severe flatulence and gas accumulation.
Rohan spends long periods on the toilet, struggling with severe constipation and painful lower abdominal muscle spasms.
Rohan's fecal elimination is highly irregular, often delayed by a full week, causing toxic backup and compaction in his terminal colon.
